prompt:
Add OS-level memory protection so any nix build (sudo'd, agent-launched, manual, doesn't matter) is safe to run alongside an interactive desktop session.

## Why
Background context (don't put this all in the PR — just enough for reviewers to understand): PRs #515/#516 added per-unit caps to `nixos-upgrade.service` and `cosmo-rebuild.service`, and per-process caps via the `cosmo-rebuild` wrapper. Those work, but they only protect specific code paths. When an agent or human runs `sudo nix build` directly from a terminal, the build runs in the user's terminal scope (`user.slice/.../kitty-*.scope`) and inherits none of those caps.

The result: classic-laddie can still wedge under heavy compiles (rusty-v8, ollama-cuda, homeassistant) if the build is invoked outside our existing wrappers. The wedge mechanism is specifically swap-in stalls in `shmem_swapin_folio` exceeding `hung_task_timeout` (122s) when the kernel evicts other resident processes' pages to disk swap to make room for the build.

This PR shifts the strategy from 'wrap each build path' to 'the OS guarantees safety regardless of who runs what.' After this lands, local builds become safe by construction; the `cosmo-rebuild` wrapper becomes nice-to-have ergonomics rather than load-bearing safety.

## Change to make
Add to `modules/common/system.nix`, alongside the existing OOM/memory-pressure protections (the `vm.swappiness` / `systemd.oomd` block):

```nix
# zram-backed compressed swap. Swap-in becomes in-memory zstd
# decompression instead of NVMe I/O, eliminating the shmem_swapin_folio
# D-state hang that wedged classic-laddie 2026-04-29 / 2026-04-29 (hung_task_timeout
# fires after 122s of disk swap-in queueing; zram swap-in is microseconds).
zramSwap = {
  enable = lib.mkDefault true;
  algorithm = "zstd";
  memoryPercent = lib.mkDefault 50;
};

# Reserve a memory floor for the interactive session. Builds, services,
# and containers (in system.slice) can be reclaimed before user.slice
# pages are. Soft reservation — user.slice can still use more than this.
systemd.slices."user".sliceConfig.MemoryMin = lib.mkDefault "8G";

# Make systemd-oomd's response to system.slice memory pressure aggressive
# enough to actually kill runaway builds before they wedge the box.
# user.slice is unaffected (only system.slice is targeted), so games /
# Chrome / the compositor are never killed by this rule.
systemd.oomd = {
  extraConfig.DefaultMemoryPressureDurationSec = lib.mkDefault "5s";
};
systemd.slices."system".sliceConfig = {
  ManagedOOMMemoryPressure = lib.mkDefault "kill";
  ManagedOOMMemoryPressureLimit = lib.mkDefault "70%";
};
```

All values use `lib.mkDefault` so hosts with different needs (e.g., microVM build workers like klaus-worker-0 where 8G MemoryMin would be invalid on a 4GB VM) can override.

## CRITICAL: no local nix tooling
- No `nixos-rebuild`, no `nix build` (even `--dry-run`), no `nix flake check`, no `nix eval`.
- The protections this PR adds aren't active on the running system yet — they only take effect after the host activates a generation built from this PR. So the same wedge risk applies during validation. CI does dry-run builds on a runner; that's the validation gate.
- (Once this lands and classic-laddie reboots, future cosmo PRs will be free to use local validation. But not this one.)

## Investigation step before editing
- Read `modules/common/system.nix` in full to understand existing structure and where the OOM-related block lives.
- Confirm `zramSwap` isn't already configured somewhere else (grep `zramSwap` across the repo). If it is, this PR adjusts the existing config rather than introducing a duplicate.
- Confirm no host already sets `systemd.slices` for user/system slices in a way that would conflict.

## PR description requirements
- The mechanism: swap-in stalls + hung_task_timeout, not OOM kills (be specific so reviewers understand why `MemoryMax` alone wouldn't have helped).
- The architectural shift: from per-path wrapping (#515/#516) to OS-guaranteed safety. Note that the `cosmo-rebuild` wrapper remains useful but is no longer load-bearing.
- Per-knob justification:
  - zram: eliminates the specific D-state hang mechanism (disk swap-in queueing). The actual root-cause fix.
  - MemoryMin on user.slice: policy statement that the interactive session is sacred; protects gaming, browsers, compositor against any runaway in system.slice.
  - Aggressive oomd on system.slice (only): kills the offender, never the user. 5s averaging window means transient spikes don't trigger kills.
- Note that all values are `lib.mkDefault` so non-desktop hosts can opt out (klaus-worker-0 specifically).
- Note that this enables a follow-up: removing the in-prompt 'no local nix tooling' rule from agent dispatch (and documenting the new state in AGENTS.md).

## Acceptance
- `modules/common/system.nix` updated with the three blocks above (or coherently merged with existing structure)
- All knobs use `lib.mkDefault`
- PR description covers the mechanism + architectural shift + per-knob justification
- CI green
